FS!QRが目指すファイル共有の新しい形: A New Approach to File Sharing — The FS!QR Vision
FS!QRの基本コンセプトと設計思想を詳しく解説。QRコードによる直感的な共有体: An in-depth look at FS!QR's core concept and design philosophy: an intuitive QR-code sharing experience, a design focused on temporary sharing, and a simple new approach to file sharing with access controlled by ID and password.
FS!QRの基本的な考え方 | サービスコンセプトと設計思想: The Core Philosophy of FS!QR | Service Concept and Design
FS!QRの最大の特徴は、QRコードを活用したシンプルな共有プロセスです。ファイ: FS!QR's defining feature is a simple, QR-code-driven sharing process. Upload a file and a QR code is generated automatically; recipients simply scan it with their smartphone to access the content. This eliminates the need to manually type URLs or wrestle with complex sharing links, making intuitive file exchange possible in both educational and business contexts.
FS!QRの機能は、教育機関とビジネス現場での実際のニーズを反映して設計されてい: FS!QR's features are built around the real-world needs of educational institutions and workplaces. From distributing class materials and sharing group project deliverables to handing out training documents at a company, each scenario is handled with as few steps as possible for maximum impact. The group sharing feature — designed for multi-person file exchange — and the note feature that enables real-time information sharing are particularly innovative, reflecting the collaborative ways people learn and work today.
FS!QRの設計思想や技術的な考え方について、開発背景とコンセプトを詳しく解説し: An in-depth look at the thinking behind FS!QR — its development background, design philosophy, and technical approach.
FS!QRは、利用者のプライバシーと信頼を最優先に考えた運営を行っています。デー: FS!QR is operated with user privacy and trust as the top priorities. We strive to be as transparent as possible about data-handling policies, security measures, and system specifications, creating an environment where users can engage with the service confidently. By actively welcoming user feedback and pursuing continuous improvement, we aim to build a file-sharing service that evolves with the times.
FS!QRは、単なるファイル共有ツールを超えて、持続可能なデジタル社会の実現に貢: FS!QR aspires to be more than a file-sharing tool — it aims to contribute to a sustainable digital society. We are committed to using technology for the broader good: reducing environmental footprint through automatic data deletion, lowering barriers to entry with a simple design, and sharing knowledge in the spirit of open source. These values are what set FS!QR apart from similar services.
FS!QRは、現代のデジタル社会において「簡単でID・パスワードでアクセスを制限: FS!QR is a web service built to make file sharing simple in today's digital world, while allowing access to be limited by ID and password. Traditional file-sharing methods often require installing a dedicated app or navigating complex settings. FS!QR takes a different approach: everything works through a browser, giving anyone an intuitive file-sharing experience with no setup required.
FS!QRは「永続的な保存」ではなく「一時的な共有」に特化した設計を採用していま: FS!QR is designed around temporary sharing rather than permanent storage. Uploaded files are automatically deleted after a set period, preventing unnecessary data from accumulating on the server. This philosophy reflects a deliberate balance between privacy and usability, creating an environment where users can share files with genuine peace of mind.
QRコードによる直感的な共有体験: An Intuitive Sharing Experience with QR Codes
ファイル共有における最重要課題は、セキュリティと利便性の両立です。FS!QRでは: Balancing security with ease of use is the central challenge in file sharing. FS!QR addresses this by encrypting uploaded files at rest and pairing that with ID-and-password access control. HTTPS encryption further protects data in transit, preventing third-party eavesdropping or tampering. All of this works behind the scenes, so users enjoy secure, access-controlled file sharing without ever dealing with complex settings.

QRコードで一時共有する仕組みと、向いている場面: How Temporary QR-Code Sharing Works and When It Fits
QRコードを使う一時的なファイル共有が、どのような流れで動くのかを利用者の視点で: This guide explains from the user's perspective how temporary file sharing with a QR code works. It covers browser-side encryption, sharing expiry, and data that is not a good fit, within the scope of FS!QR's implementation.
This guide explains from the user's perspective how temporary file sharing with a QR code works, including browser-side encryption, sharing expiry, and data that is not a good fit.
QRコード ファイル共有, 一時共有, ブラウザ暗号化, AES-GCM, 共有: QR-code file sharing, temporary sharing, browser encryption, AES-GCM, sharing expiry, FS QR
「ファイルを一時的に渡したいが、相手にアカウントを作ってもらうほどではない」とい: Sometimes you need to hand over a file temporarily, but it is not worth asking the other person to create an account. FS!QR is designed for this short handoff using a URL or QR code and a password. Rather than presenting features as advertising, this guide explains what happens from the start of sharing until it expires, at a level that helps users make an informed choice.
共有は「アップロード」「案内」「受け取り」に分かれる: Sharing Has Three Parts: Upload, Guide, and Receive
送信側はブラウザでファイルを選び、保存期間とパスワードを確認してアップロードしま: The sender selects a file in the browser, checks the retention period and password, and uploads it. When the upload is complete, the URL, sharing ID, password, and QR code to give the recipient are displayed. The recipient scans the QR code or opens the URL and enters the password as instructed. The sender and recipient do not need to have the same screen open at the same time.
この流れが役立つのは、対面の会議で画面にQRコードを表示する場合、PCからスマー: This flow is useful when displaying a QR code on a screen in an in-person meeting, moving a photo from a PC to a smartphone, or giving materials to someone who cannot install an app. Long-term collaboration, business storage that must retain access history, and detailed permission management are better handled by dedicated storage or an organization-focused service.
QRコードはファイルそのものではなく、入口を渡す: A QR Code Shares an Entry Point, Not the File Itself
QRコードにファイルの中身が入っているわけではありません。コードには共有先へ移動: A QR code does not contain the file itself. It contains information for reaching the sharing destination, from which the recipient opens the sharing page. In practice, you are giving an entry point to anyone who can photograph the code. Before displaying it on a meeting-room screen or public stream, consider who can scan it and send the password through a separate channel if necessary.
URLとパスワードを同じ公開チャットへ貼れば、QRコードを使っていても保護は弱く: If you post the URL and password in the same public chat, protection is weakened even when you use a QR code. A QR code saves typing; it does not replace checking the recipient or managing authentication information.
FS!QRのQRコード共有では、ブラウザ側でファイルを暗号化してからサーバーへ保: With FS!QR QR-code sharing, the file is encrypted in the browser before it is stored on the server, and the connection is protected by HTTPS. Under the current specification, Group files and Note text are not stored encrypted on the server. Protection differs by feature even within the same service, so choose a different sharing method for highly confidential content.
暗号化されていても、受信者が保存したコピー、スクリーンショット、誤った相手へ伝え: Encryption does not erase a copy saved by the recipient, a screenshot, or a password sent to the wrong person. An expiry setting shortens the life of the share on the server; it does not remotely erase the recipient's device after receipt.
共有前に判断する三つの質問: Three Questions to Answer Before Sharing
受信者は誰か。URLやQRコードを見られる人を説明できるか。: Who is the recipient, and can you explain who will be able to see the URL or QR code?
相手が必要とする期間はどれくらいか。最長の設定が本当に必要か。: How long does the other person need it, and is the longest available setting really necessary?
保存・監査・本人確認が必要なデータではないか。必要なら組織で承認された保管場所を: Does the data require retention, auditing, or identity verification? If so, use a storage location approved by your organization.
この三つに答えられないままアップロードを始めないことが、FS!QRを安全に使う最: The most important prerequisite for using FS!QR safely is not starting the upload until you can answer these three questions. Do not skip selecting an appropriate storage location just because sharing is convenient.
実際に試すなら、機密性のないファイルから始める: If You Try It, Start with a Non-Confidential File
初回は公開しても問題のない小さな画像やテスト用PDFを使い、送信側で表示される情: For the first test, use a small image or test PDF that is safe to expose, and check what the sender sees, where the recipient saves it, and what appears after expiry. Verify the behavior in your own environment before sharing real materials. If you need to record the result or the recipient's completion, prepare a separate work procedure for that.
